retry_reason: Failure occurs when no matching device is found or no ACPI companion exists; these are lookup failures, not refcount operation failures. Retrying may succeed if the device appears later, but the function is not a ref-count wrapper and retry semantics are not part of its contract.

judge.reason: The function's primary purpose is to retrieve an ACPI device pointer via bus_find_device and ACPI_COMPANION, not to manage reference counts. It temporarily gets a reference on the platform device but immediately releases it via __free(put_device). No refcount is acquired on the returned acpi_device; the caller receives a raw pointer without any ownership or reference. The reference-count operation is incidental and not the function's main intent.
judge.retry_reason: Failure occurs when no matching device is found or no ACPI companion exists; these are lookup failures, not refcount operation failures. Retrying may succeed if the device appears later, but the function is not a ref-count wrapper and retry semantics are not part of its contract.
